prd.oneLiner: Connect travel requests, itineraries and budgets, approvals, bookings, expense reconciliation, and policy reporting.
prd.goal: Reduce travel approval and booking-confirmation lead time by 25% and reduce out-of-policy spend.
prd.whyNow: Travel requests, approvals, bookings, and expense reconciliation live in separate tools, making changes and policy checks difficult.
prd.problem: Travel requests, approvals, bookings, and expense reconciliation live in separate tools, making changes and policy checks difficult.
prd.solution: Connect travel requests, itineraries and budgets, approvals, bookings, expense reconciliation, and policy reporting.
prd.alternatives: Email, chat, spreadsheets, and manual ledgers
prd.differentiator: Manage intake, processing, and operations data as one connected source of truth.

targets.name: Employee requesting and reconciling a business trip
targets.needs: Quickly register needed work and know its status.
targets.pain: Travel requests, approvals, bookings, and expense reconciliation live in separate tools, making changes and policy checks difficult.

targets.name: General affairs and finance travel coordinator
targets.needs: Manage priority and history and review operations metrics.
targets.pain: Travel requests, approvals, bookings, and expense reconciliation live in separate tools, making changes and policy checks difficult.

scenarios.text: Employee requesting and reconciling a business trip completes travel request, itinerary, and budget submission and checks progress.
General affairs and finance travel coordinator reviews travel spend and policy-compliance reporting by department and region.

prd.northStar: Share of operations requests completed on time

kpis.name: On-time processing rate
kpis.target: Reduce travel approval and booking-confirmation lead time by 25% and reduce out-of-policy spend.
kpis.method: Monthly aggregation of completion history against target dates

kpis.name: Operations visibility
kpis.method: Share of registered requests connected to operations reporting

inScope: Travel request, itinerary, and budget submission
Traveler, itinerary, approval, booking, and expense-status lookup
Travel approval, booking review, change, and post-trip reconciliation handling
Travel spend and policy-compliance reporting by department and region
nonGoals: Replacing adjacent systems such as accounting or payroll
assumptions: User and organization data are available through company SSO.
Operators manage policy and classification criteria.
risks: Weak initial classification can cause incorrect processing.
Stale source data can reduce operational trust.
openQuestions: What defines urgent processing and approval?
What are the post-completion verification and reopen rules?
constraints: State, owner, and priority changes require audit logs.
